name: Basic Muffins and Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the baking powder, dry milk, and salt into the sifted flour.
2. Sift all dry ingredients together until well mixed.
3. Cut or mix fat into the flour mixture until all particles are thoroughly coated and the mixture resembles coarse cornmeal.
4. Store the mix in a jar or can with a tight lid. It can be measured into 2 cup amounts and placed in plastic bags or jars where it will be ready for use. It may be stored at room temperature for 6 weeks.
5. Vary the liquids: since milk solids are already in the mix, more milk is not needed. You can use fruit juice, water, or meat broth for the liquid called for in a recipe.
6. Basic biscuits require 2 cups of the mix and 1/2 cup of water. Add water to the mix and stir 20-25 times.
7. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead 10-15 times.
8. Roll to a thickness of 1/2 inch and cut with a knife into pocket shapes or use a biscuit cutter to make circles.
9. Place in a preheated oven and bake for 5 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: Sausage biscuits require 2 1/4 cups of the packaged biscuit baking mix, 8 cooked sausage links, knead dough, roll out on a floured surface, cut into circles, lay one sausage in the center, wrap dough around, and pinch to seal. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden brown.
Jelly biscuits require flattening unbaked biscuits with the heel of your hand, placing 1/2 teaspoon jelly in the center, folding over, and pinching edges together to seal. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden.
Pimento cheese bread requires mixing 2 cups of packaged biscuit baking mix, 1 beaten egg, 1/2 cup grated cheddar, 2/3 cup milk, and optional diced pimento. Mix until a soft dough forms, spoon into pockets, and bake for 10 minutes or until golden.
